column1: SECTION B — NBFDS ACCEPTABLE DISCLOSURE METHODS  (7 CFR 66.100–66.116)

column4: Requirements

column2: Words 'Bioengineered food' or 'Contains a bioengineered food ingredient' printed on label
column4: Font ≥ same size as other required label text; placed prominently
column7: Simplest and most defensible. Recommended for B2B ingredients destined for finished foods requiring disclosure.

column2: USDA-designed sun/smiley symbol (AMS official BE symbol) on packaging
column4: Symbol must be legible; min size per USDA AMS spec; approved colour renderings only
column7: Growing consumer recognition. Requires AMS symbol file. Works well on retail packs.

column2: QR code or URL linking to online disclosure page maintained by manufacturer
column4: Must include text: 'Scan here for more food information'; landing page must be AMS-compliant
column7: Allows detailed information. URL/QR must be maintained. Requires digital infrastructure.

column2: Phone number + 'Text [word] to [number] for bioengineered food information'
column4: Response must deliver disclosure; system maintained
column6:
Small manufacturers
(<$2.5M annual receipts) may use SMS-only
column7: Least common. Only viable for small brands or foodservice packaging.

column2: Small manufacturers (<$2.5M receipts) may use electronic or text message disclosure only for 1 year additional transition; very small manufacturers exempt.
column4: Annual receipts threshold checked against prior year
column7: B2B ingredient suppliers are NOT exempt — disclosure obligation applies to the finished food manufacturer.

column1: SECTION C — GMO TESTING METHODS & THRESHOLDS

column3: Detection Limit
column4: Scope
Screen for presence/absence of specific GMO construct (e.g. 35S promoter, NOS terminator, event-specific)
column6: No NBFDS threshold — any detection triggers disclosure obligation
column7: No de minimis — any detection = labelling required under Reg EC 1829/2003

column4: Quantifies % GMO content per event; required for EU labelling threshold compliance
column6: No NBFDS % threshold — disclosure triggered by presence, not quantity
column7: ≤0.9% per approved event: adventitious/technically unavoidable presence exemption

column4: Detects novel proteins expressed by GM construct (Bt Cry proteins, CP4 EPSPS)
column6: Useful for initial screen; confirm with PCR
column7: Not accepted as standalone for EU labelling compliance — PCR required for confirmation

column3: 0.01–0.1% depending on event
column4: Identifies specific approved GM event (e.g. MON810, NK603, RR soybean)
column6: Required for EU import compliance and Non-GMO Project verification
column7: Required: must show <0.9% of specific authorised event

column2: Non-GMO Project Standard v14+; USDA NOP (Organic)
column3: 0.1–0.9% (scheme-dependent)
column4: Segregation + chain-of-custody CoA from field through processing. Physical audits at each step.
column6: Non-GMO Project: PCR test per lot + IP CoA required. Sets ≤0.9% threshold.
column7: Organic certification: ≤0% intentional — accidental contamination case-by-case

column1: SECTION D — REQUIREMENTS TO SUBSTANTIATE A 'NON-GMO' CLAIM ON FINISHED PRODUCT

column3: Detail
Written statement from ingredient supplier confirming ingredient is not bioengineered and not derived from BE sources
column6: Minimum — not sufficient alone for verified Non-GMO claim

column3: Chain-of-custody document from seed/harvest through processing confirming physical segregation from GMO materials at each step
column6: Required by Non-GMO Project and for meaningful claim substantiation

column3: Per-lot PCR test showing ≤0.9% GMO content (ISO 21570 or Non-GMO Project approved lab). Annual contract testing acceptable for low-risk ingredients.
column6: ISO/IEC 17025 accredited lab required. Non-GMO Project mandates testing per scheme v14.

column3: Third-party verification by Non-GMO Project (nongmoproject.org). Covers supply chain, testing, and annual renewal. Most recognised US consumer standard.
column6: Gold standard for US retail Non-GMO claims. Required by most major US retailers for Non-GMO shelf label.

column3: USDA NOP certified organic products may not intentionally use GMO. De facto Non-GMO status — organic seal substitutes Non-GMO claim.
column6: Covers GMO by definition. No separate GMO test required if USDA Organic certified.

column3: If ingredient is soy or corn-derived AND Non-GMO claimed: ensure allergen declaration is consistent with non-GMO sourcing and IP program
column6: FALCPA + NBFDS compliance must be aligned. Non-GMO soy lecithin: still requires soy allergen disclosure.
